<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Konpira Gongen]][[es:Konpira Gongen]][[ja:金毘羅権現]][[simple:Kompira]][[zh:金毘羅權現]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Konpira Gongen]][[es:Konpira Gongen]][[ja:金毘羅権現]][[simple:Kompira]][[zh:金毘羅權現]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Konpira Gongen]][[es:Konpira Gongen]][[ja:金毘羅権現]][[simple:Kompira]][[zh:金毘羅權現]]



{{Short description|Japanese god of the Shugendō sect}}
{{Multiple issues|
{{Expand Japanese|金毘羅権現|date=April 2021}}
{{Expand Japanese|金刀比羅神社|date=November 2023}}
{{Expand Japanese|宮比羅|date=November 2023}}
{{more citations needed|date=October 2023}}
}}

{{Infobox deity
| type = Shinto
| name = Konpira Gongen
| deity_of =God of seafaring and sailors; {{ill|water transport|qid=Q155930|1=Maritime transport}}| member_of = 
| image =Image:Konpira Daigongen.jpg; File:Konpira Daigongen.jpg| alt = 
| caption = Statue of Konpira Gongen, [[:en:Musée Guimet|Musée Guimet]]
| other_names = Konpira Daigongen (金毘羅大権現)
| script_name = Japanese
| script = 金毘羅権現
| affiliation = [[Acala]] (''[[Honji suijaku|honji]]'')<br/>[[Ōmononushi]] ([[Shinbutsu-shūgō|conflated with]])<br/>{{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|qid=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku|1=Emperor Sutoku}}
| cult_center = [[Kotohira-gū]] ({{ill|Kotohira, Kagawa|en|Kotohira, Kagawa|qid=Q1271483|lt=Kotohira|1=Kotohira, Kagawa}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|qid=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}})
| abode = 
| planet = 
| mantra = ''[[Om|On]] Kubiraya {{ill|Svaha|en|Svaha|qid=Q2614321|lt=sowaka|1=Svaha}}''
| equivalent1_type = Buddhist
| equivalent1 = {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|en|Bhaisajyaguru|qid=Q854773|lt=Bhaisajyaguru|1=Bhaisajyaguru}}
| venerated_in = {{ill|Shugendō|qid=Q1483875|1=Shugendō}}
}}

'''Konpira [[Gongen]]''' (金毘羅権現) is a Japanese god of the [[Shugendō]] sect originating in the mountain [[:en:Kotohira|Kotohira]] of {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|qid=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. He is the god of merchant sailors. He is worshipped at {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}

According to legend {{ill|Konpira Gongen|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=}} came into existence when a priest at {{ill|Matsuo-ji|lt=Matsuo-ji|lt=Matsuo Temple (Kagawa Prefecture Kotohira Town)|lt=Matsuo Temple|ja|松尾寺 (香川県琴平町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuo Temple (Kagawa Prefecture Kotohira Town)|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} summoned the Ganges deity {{ill|Kumbhīra|ja|宮比羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumbhīra|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.<ref>https://www.researchgate.net/publication/334586970_Kagawa_A_Guide_to_Sanuki_Gateway_to_Shikoku</ref>

{{ill|Kumbhīra|ja|宮比羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumbhīra|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was said to have been present at the preaching of the Mahāsamaya Sutta.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Kumbhīra |url=https://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/ku/kumbhiira.htm |access-date=2023-10-17 |website=www.palikanon.com}}</ref> He is a Ganges crocodile god.<ref>{{Cite web |last=D |first=John |date=2013-06-01 |title=Hinduism 2): Kompira |url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2013/06/01/hindu-connections-2-kompira/ |access-date=2023-10-17 |website=Green Shinto |language=en-GB}}</ref>

He is identified with the first of the twelve spirit messengers emanating from {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|en|Bhaisajyaguru|qid=Q854773|lt=Bhaisajyaguru|1=Bhaisajyaguru}}.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book |last=FAURE |first=BERNARD |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.2307/j.ctv1k3np30 |title=Rage and Ravage |date=2021-12-31 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-8936-4 |doi=10.2307/j.ctv1k3np30}}</ref>{{rp|52}}

Alongside many other water deities like {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|qid=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi|1=Watatsumi}}, [[Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi]], [[Munakata-sanjojin]], {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|Ebisu (mythology)|qid=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu|1=Ebisu (mythology)}}, and [[Gozu Tennō]] his cult became very significant in medieval times.<ref name=":0" />{{rp|316}}

==Overview==
[[Yoshida Kanetomo]] said that Susanoo, the Shinto god of sea and storms, is the same as the Indian god Khumbīra. This god watches over Vulture Peak, a place in {{ill|Buddhist mythology|en|Buddhist mythology|qid=Q2628860|lt=Buddhist mythology|1=Buddhist mythology}}...<ref name=":0" />{{rp|172}}

As time passed, people in Japan began to see Konpira as a guardian of their Buddhist religion. They placed Konpira in a shrine on Zōzusan mountain in [[Shikoku]]. This mountain was very important for Shugendō followers by the end of the Heian period. Shugendō is a Japanese belief that combines mountain living, Shinto, and Buddhism. On this mountain, Konpira was given a new name, Konpira Gongen...<ref name=":0" />{{rp|172}}

=== Associations ===
Konpira is connected to gods of water, like dragons and nāga kings. These connections make him a protector of people at sea and the paths they take over the water. This is especially true for the waters inside Japan's coast. In a special part of Buddhism, people honor Konpira as one of twelve powerful spirit leaders. These leaders are linked to twelve directions and twelve animals from the Chinese calendar. Different stories match Konpira to different directions. But most often, he is linked to the north...<ref name=":0" />{{rp|172}}

Sometimes Konpira is identified with gods like Ōkuninushi or {{ill|Shinra Myōjin|en|Shinra Myōjin|qid=Q22127349|lt=Shinra Myōjin|1=Shinra Myōjin}}. ...<ref name=":0" />{{rp|172}} But the most recent identification is with [[Ōmononushi]]. His identification with [[Ōmononushi]] may seem strange at first since [[Ōmononushi]] is a deity of mountains and snakes, while he is a water and navigatonal deity, but the logic behind it is that mountains are often used for navigation in the sea, so mountain deities help sailors..<ref name=":0" />{{rp|317}}

=== In Literature ===
A Buddhist text "[[Sange yōryakki]]" talks about Buddha placing twelve gods on Eagle Peak. One of these gods is Konpira. Here, Konpira is seen as a very important god in Japan. He is thought to be the divine child of Susanoo no Mikoto. People believed that a monk named Saichō came back safely from China because he prayed to Susanoo...<ref name=":0" />{{rp|172}}

In later books, like "Jindai no maki kaden kikigaki" by Koretari, there are more stories. They say Susanoo visited not just Japan but also China and India. These stories suggest that the Japanese gods could be versions of Indian gods. This idea turns the usual story around, with Japanese gods being seen in other countries' stories...<ref name=":0" />{{rp|172}}

==Kotohira Shrines==

{{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|simple|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} are a kind of {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in Japan. They were originally dedicated to the syncretic deity [[Konpira Gongen]], but in the Meiji restorration due to [[Shinbutsu bunri]] they were dedicated to [[Ōmononushi]]. Their head shrine is [[Kotohira-gū]]..<ref>{{Cite web |last=SHIKOKU |first=Organization for Promotion of Tourism in |title=Kotohira-gu Shrine |url=https://shikoku-tourism.com/en/see-and-do/10078 |access-date=2023-09-17 |website=Tourism SHIKOKU |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="konpira-shrine:1">{{Cite web |title=Kompirasan |url=https://www.japan-guide.com/e/e5451.html |access-date=2023-09-18 |website=www.japan-guide.com |language=en}}</ref> They are dedicated to seafaring and protecting sailors.<ref name="konpira-shrine:1" />

They are part of the famous Inahachikonten Shrines (稲八金天神社)  of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] alongside {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|qid=Q514480|lt=Inari shrine|1=Inari shrine}}, [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|simple|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and [[Tenmangū|Tenmangu Shrine]].

=== History ===
According to legend {{ill|Konpira Gongen|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=}} came into existence when a priest at {{ill|Matsuo-ji|lt=Matsuo-ji|lt=Matsuo Temple (Kagawa Prefecture Kotohira Town)|lt=Matsuo Temple|ja|松尾寺 (香川県琴平町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuo Temple (Kagawa Prefecture Kotohira Town)|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} summoned the Ganges deity {{ill|Kumbhīra|ja|宮比羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumbhīra|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.<ref>https://www.researchgate.net/publication/334586970_Kagawa_A_Guide_to_Sanuki_Gateway_to_Shikoku</ref><ref name="konpira-shrine:2">{{Cite book |last=Faure |first=Bernard |date=2021-12-16 |title=Rage and Ravage |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824889364 |doi=10.1515/9780824889364|isbn=978-0-8248-8936-4 |s2cid=245466185 }}</ref>{{rp|172}} styarting as a hostile sea monster the deity was placated and became a god of navigation.<ref name="konpira-shrine:2" />{{rp|353}} The deity is perceived as perhaps being a deification of a sea serpent called a {{ill|Wani (dragon)|en|Wani (dragon)|qid=Q20049265|lt=wani|1=Wani (dragon)}}.<ref name="konpira-shrine:2" />{{rp|437}}

[[Kotohira-gū]] is located close to and strongly historically associated with {{ill|Matsuo-ji|lt=Matsuo-ji|lt=Matsuo Temple (Kagawa Prefecture Kotohira Town)|lt=Matsuo Temple|ja|松尾寺 (香川県琴平町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuo Temple (Kagawa Prefecture Kotohira Town)|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=Kotohira: The Area Around the Kotohira-gu Shrine, Where the Old Townscape Remains to This Day |url=https://www.fun-japan.jp/en/articles/10547 |access-date=2023-10-17 |website=FUN! JAPAN International |language=en}}</ref>

In 1889 due to the [[Meiji Restoration]] the head temple was renamed from Konpira temple to [[:en:Kotohira-gu|Kotohira-gu]]..<ref name="konpira-shrine:2" />{{rp|437}}

===List of Kotohira Shrines===

* [[Kotohira-gū]]
* {{ill|Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine|ja|大綱金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine|1=Otsuna Konpira Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q117062765}}
* {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kuji)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (久慈市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Kuji City)|qq=}}
* {{ill|Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|1=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|1=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}
* {{ill|Kotohira-gū (Tokyo Port Area)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira-gū (Tokyo Port Area)|1=Konpira Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11646485}}
* {{ill|Kotohira-gū Tokyo Branch|ja|金刀比羅宮東京分社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira-gū Tokyo Branch|qq=}}
* {{ill|Kotohira-gū Matsuyama Branch|ja|金刀比羅宮松山分社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira-gū Matsuyama Branch|1=Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11646484}}
* {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Joetsu City)|ja|琴平神社 (上越市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Joetsu City)|qq=}}
* {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kohei Town)|ja|琴平神社 (古平町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Kohei Town)|1=Kotohira Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28685653}}
* {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)|ja|琴平神社 (土佐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)|qq=}}
* {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|琴平神社 (川崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Kawasaki City)|1=Kotohira Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30932918}}
* {{ill|Gozu Tennō Faith in Tatebayashi|lt=Kotohira Shrine (Tatebayashi City)|ja|琴平神社 (館林市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō Faith in Tatebayashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 館林の牛頭天王信仰}}
* {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kyotango City)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (京丹後市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Kyotango City)|1=Konpira Shrine (Kyotango)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q56523409}}
* {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Taito)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}}
* {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City Kawauchi Town)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City Kawauchi Town)|qq=}}
* {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|qq=}}
* {{ill|Nemuro Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City)|qq=}}
* {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Naruto City)|qq=}}
* {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}}
* {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Nagareyama City)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (流山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Nagareyama City)|1=Kotohira Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97311700}}
* {{ill|Konpira-gū (Tama City)|ja|金比羅宮 (多摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira-gū (Tama City)|1=Konpiragū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11647333}}
* {{ill|Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)|ja|金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)|qq=}}
* {{ill|Bessho Kotohira Shrine|ja|別所琴平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Kotohira Shrine|1=Bessho Kotohira Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11396790}}
* {{ill|Miyoshi Shrine (Sapporo City)|ja|三吉神社 (札幌市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Shrine (Sapporo City)|qq=}}
* {{ill|Yasui Konpira-gū|ja|安井金比羅宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasui Konpira-gū|1=Yasui Kompira-gu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11450246}}
* {{ill|Matsuo-ji|lt=Matsuo-ji|lt=Matsuo Temple (Kagawa Prefecture Kotohira Town)|ja|松尾寺 (香川県琴平町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuo Temple (Kagawa Prefecture Kotohira Town)|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
* {{ill|Ryūtōsan Shrine|en|Ryūtōsan Shrine|qid=Q11180716|lt=Ryūtōzan Shrine|1=Ryūtōsan Shrine}}
* {{ill|Sapporo Hachimangū|en|Sapporo Hachimangū|qid=Q28689612|lt=Sapporo Hachimangū|1=Sapporo Hachimangū}}

==See also==
*''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]''
*''[[Honji suijaku]]''
*[[Kotohira-gū]]
*{{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|qid=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku|1=Emperor Sutoku}}

== References ==
{{reflist}}

{{shinto shrines}}

{{Japan-reli-stub}}
{{Kotohira Faith}}

{{translated page|ja|金毘羅権現|version=104322926|comment=Imported full ja history}}



[[en:Konpira Gongen]]

<!-- for alternate text of the title image per [[WP:ALT]] --><!-- or | abodes = --><!-- or | world = --><!-- sourrce says Kametomo but I think thisis almost cerrtainly a typo -->
<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->
[[es:Konpira Gongen]]
[[ja:金毘羅権現]]
[[zh:金毘羅權現]]
<!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis-->
[[es:Konpira Gongen]]
[[ja:金毘羅権現]]
[[zh:金毘羅權現]]

[[en:Konpira Gongen]]
{{wikidata link|Q167077}}
[[Category:Acala]]
[[Category:Gongen]]
[[Category:Japanese deities]]
[[Category:Japanese gods]]
[[Category:Japanese religious terminology]]
[[Category:Kotohira shrines]]
[[Category:River deities]]
[[Category:Sea and river deities]]
[[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]]
[[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]]
[[Category:Shugendō deities]]
[[Category:Tengu]]
[[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]]
[[Category:Vaiśravaṇa]]
[[Category:Water transport]]
[[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
[[Category:Need translation]]
